items.title: Top 10 best music gear so far in 2023
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while preserving all factual information and required links.
items.excerptBefore: Explore the top 10 music gear highlights of 2023, from innovative instruments to cutting-edge tech. Unleash your musical potential today!
items.excerptAfter: Explore the top 10 music gear of 2023, featuring innovative tools for musicians, including the Soundbrenner Core Steel and Genki Wave Ring.
items.seoTitleBefore: Top 10 best music gear so far in 2023
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Explore the top 10 music gear highlights of 2023, from innovative instruments to cutting-edge tech. Unleash your musical potential today!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover the top 10 music gear of 2023, including innovative tools like the Soundbrenner Core Steel and Genki Wave Ring. Join our giveaway worth $2500!
items.beforeBody:
2023 is proving to be an exciting year for musicians. From cutting-edge tech to beloved classics, the world of music gear is buzzing with options. In this blog, we'll walk you through the top 10 pieces of gear that have caught our attention this year.

P.s. We at Soundbrenner are doing a massive giveaway (worth US $2500) with all the gear mentioned below! You can sign up for it here. (The giveaway ends on the 14th of Nov, 2023 at 11:59 PM PST)

Join giveaway!

1. Soundbrenner's Core Steel

Buy now

Soundbrenner Core Steel is a vibrating metronome that offers a revolutionary way to enhance your practice/gig sessions. Instead of the usual audio clicks, it allows you to feel the rhythm with vibrations seven times stronger than the average smartphone. The user-friendly app lets you easily customize rhythmic elements such as BPM, time signature, and accents.

Whether you strap it to your wrist, arm, or ankle, the versatility of this metronome ensures that you physically feel each beat. Additional features like precision tuning, dB meters, and smart notifications make this metronome the ultimate music gear for every musician.

2. Genki Wave Ring

Buy now

Ever dreamed of having a musical instrument at your fingertips? Literally? 

Then the Genki Wave Ring is for you. 

This wearable MIDI controller is motion-controlled, has six unique gestures, and is Eurorack compatible. You can control various functions with just a press of a button, making your music production practice more fluid. 

And the best part? 

It's also perfect for live performances, letting you control your music seamlessly with the flick of a finger.

3. Minuendo Lossless Earplugs

Buy now

Minuendo uses membrane technology that mimics nature and behaves like a second eardrum. For earplugs with attenuation, the attenuation is normally fixed. Minuendo lossless earplugs’ uniqueness comes with its built-in attenuators, which allow you to easily switch the attenuation.

This technology is completely passive, which makes Minuendo earplugs very convenient and practical to use. The level of attenuation ranges from 7dB to 25dB, so you can easily switch between more or less sound depending on your situation.

Key features:

  • Adjustable attenuation from 7-25dB
  • Variable passive Hi-Fi filter
  • Sleek design for everyday wear
  • Comfortable for long durations of use
  • Includes 11 sets of ear tips for you to fit your perfect fit

4. Smartphone Studio Microphone

Buy now

Compatible with any device, the Smartphone Studio Mic is everything you need to record music from your home. Get studio quality recording (up to 24-bit/9kHz), compatibility with iOS, Android, macOS and Windows, and built-in gain control and direct monitoring. It's also completely powered by your device, so no charging is required at all! Its ease of use makes it one of the best music gear for any musician out there!

5. STOMP Bluetooth Page Turner Pedal (Limited edition)

Buy now

As tablets become more popular for reading sheet music, consider amping up that convenience with a STOMP Bluetooth page-turner. With this foot-switch, keep your hands free to play your instrument, instead of stressing out about the 0.5 seconds you have to turn the page. You can also program this pedal to control your DAW or trigger backing tracks. A perfect gift for any gigging musician.

  • Enjoying this article? Check out our 10 best gifts for guitarists

6. Roadie 3 Tuner

Buy now

For guitarists who want a tuner, string winder, and metronome all in one - the Roadie 3 tuner has your back. It comes with a variety of built-in alternate tunings, is compact and wireless, and is pretty simple to use!

This next-generation automatic tuner will help you find your sound fast with both quicker rotation, and enhanced accuracy so you'll never play out of tune again.

7. TouchMe

Buy now

TouchMe allows you to connect to objects in the same way as Playtron. However, this is a sensory device as it is set to react differently to the touch. Meaning, that when the area of touch and intensity changes, the sound changes too, causing the pitch to go higher or lower!

Why we love it

  • Overall great sequencer
  • Perfect amount of keys
  • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances. 
  • Explore the theremin-like sound generation potential of the TouchMe device to create dynamic and expressive music. 
  • Interact with other people in a musical creative environment.
  • Very portable (can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup)

8. Playtron

Price: US$95

The Playtron MIDI Controller offers a unique way to create your custom MIDI controller. It can connect up to 16 objects with alligator clips. This allows you to use it with several mobile apps, online synthesizers, or your favorite DAW.

From fruits to plants, and even toys, the possibilities are endless. Connect the clips from objects capable of conducting electricity to Playtron, and assign a different MIDI note (C1 to D#2 by default) to each object. Then, you can start playing and creating your unique sounds!

Why we love it

  • A unique way to make music, and you could add a very interesting/interactive aspect to your performance
  • Very affordable price for the several versatile features
  • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances. 
  • Very portable (can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup)

9. Aeros gold edition

Buy now

The Aeros Gold Edition is a musician's dream loop pedal. It offers a wealth of features, including a multi-track loop pedal with a mute switch and digital mixing, full of innovative features for importing your own backing tracks. With six stereo tracks, built-in Wi-Fi, and the ability to control your loops with hand gestures, it opens up a world of creative possibilities.

The automatic song transitions, customizable effects, and compatibility with MIDI and other devices make this pedal a powerful tool for creating complex, layered compositions and bringing your musical ideas to life.

10. Flamma FS08

Buy now

The Flamma FS08 is a versatile and compact multi-effects unit that's perfect for guitarists looking to expand their sonic horizons. It offers precise polyphonic octave effects without any distorted signal, ensuring your sound stays crystal clear.

With 7 preset slots for saving the different effect parameters, you can easily switch between your favourite settings and experiment with various tones. Its intuitive interface and easy-to-use controls make it a valuable addition to any guitarist's arsenal, whether you're recording in the studio or performing on stage.

In 2023, the music gear landscape is as alive as it will ever be with innovation and creative possibilities. From the Soundbrenner Core Steel to the Genki Wave Ring and Playtron MIDI Controller, this year's offerings cater to musicians of all kinds.

Whether you're practicing, recording, or performing, 2023's music gear has something for everyone. It's a harmonious blend of tradition and cutting-edge tech, ensuring that musicians find the perfect tools to bring their musical dreams to life. Happy playing!

If you're interested in signing up for our massive giveaway (worth US $2500) with all the gear mentioned above! You can sign up for it here. (The giveaway ends on the 14th of Nov, 2023 at 11:59 PM PST)

Join giveaway!

items.title: Dynamic range in music: The art of loudness and silence
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original factual content. All links and image URLs have been preserved.
items.excerptBefore: Explore the role of dynamic range in music to achieve the perfect balance between loudness and silence through practical techniques.
items.excerptAfter: Explore the importance of dynamic range in music, the balance of loudness and silence, and techniques to enhance your compositions for a more immersive listening experience.
items.seoTitleBefore: Dynamic range in music: The art of loudness and silence
items.seoTitleAfter: Dynamic Range in Music: Mastering Loudness and Silence
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Explore the role of dynamic range in music to achieve the perfect balance between loudness and silence through practical techniques.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Learn about dynamic range in music, its significance, and how to effectively balance loudness and silence to enhance your compositions.
items.beforeBody:
Are you struggling to achieve the right sound balance in your music compositions? Dynamic range in music is the difference between the softest and loudest parts of a song. It plays a critical role in creating an immersive listening experience.

This article will guide you through understanding and mastering dynamic range, helping you strike that perfect balance of loudness and silence for maximum musical impact. Ready to add depth to your music? Let's dive in.

Key takeaways

  • Dynamic range in music shows the change in sound from soft to loud. It adds life and feelings to a song.
  • Musicians use silence smartly to make songs stand out. Silence can add depth and emotion.
  • Keeping a good balance between loud and soft parts is key in music. Too much or too little of one can ruin the listening experience.
  • Some ways to boost dynamics are using audio compression, trying Yamaha AVENTAGE receivers, aiming for a wider dynamic range, avoiding over-compression, and making use of Loudness Normalization.

The power of dynamics and silence in music

Dynamics serve as a potent tool in conveying emotional depth in music. Delving into history, dynamics have always played a significant role in shaping musical narratives. Conversely, silence creates a striking contrast and invokes tension, thereby enhancing the overall impact of your composition.

Dynamics as a powerful expressive concept

In music, dynamics pack a punch. They bring songs to life and fill the air with feeling. Dynamics play with how loud or soft the sounds are. This change in sound can make a big impact on how we feel about the song.

It's like adding salt to food - it brings out all the flavors! Too much or too little can ruin a meal, just like in music. Balanced dynamics make songs enjoyable and engaging for us to listen to.

The history and significance of dynamic range in music

The history of dynamic range in music is very strong. Musicians have used dynamics in music for many years. They knew how to make a loud sound and a soft one. It was not until the 1600s that we started putting names to these sounds. In Italy, they came up with words like "piano" for soft and "forte" for loud.

This is why we call the large keyboard instrument a piano! It means 'soft' in Italian.

Over time, more words were added. These words let musicians know how loud or soft to play at different parts of a song. Today, dynamics are key in making music fun to listen to. They add life and emotion to songs.

When you hear your favorite song, it's often the dynamic range — those ups and downs from quiet passages to booming choruses — that makes it special.

So next time you're listening or playing music, pay close attention! Can you notice when the volume changes? Understanding this can help you enjoy your favorite tunes even more.

The impact of silence in creating contrast and tension

Silence plays a big part in music. It can make loud and soft parts stand out more. This is because silence creates contrast and tension in the song. If you let there be quiet moments, your song will feel full of life.

Using silence smartly also adds emotion to your music. You want the folks listening to feel something. Silence can help with this. Also, songs that have clear changes in level are often more fun to listen to.

They seem more alive and fresh than songs that stay at one level all the time.

Understanding dynamic range in music

Dive into the concept of dynamic range - it's not just about volume, but contrast. Grasp its critical role in shaping a song's emotional contour. Appreciate how deftly balancing loud and soft passages can evoke different reactions from your listeners.

Learn how it breathes life into your music, offering an immersive experience that keeps audiences hooked from start to finish.

Definition of dynamic range

Dynamic range is a key part of music. It shows the change in sound from soft to loud. The range is found by looking at how loud and how soft a piece can get. This measure uses decibels (dB).

Our ears can hear sounds that have about 90 dB of change. This means we can hear very quiet sounds like whispers or very loud ones like jets taking off. Different types of music use different dynamic ranges.

Pop, rock, R&B, hip-hop, and country music often keep the range small. Jazz and classical music may have big changes from soft to loud parts, though.

Importance of balancing loud and soft passages

Balancing loud and soft parts in music is key. This mix brings life to your songs. It makes the listener feel every note. If a song is too loud or too quiet, it can throw off the experience.

No one wants to listen to a song that hurts their ears because of its loudness.

Finding the right balance takes skill. You don't want sections of the song missed because they are too quiet. But you also don't want ear pain from high volume! Using dynamic range in music helps find this balance.

Keep your sound below 0 dBFS to avoid harsh noise. A good blend of soft and loud makes for great music. 

How dynamic range affects the listener's experience

Dynamic range shapes how we enjoy music. It is the gap between soft and loud sounds in a song. When this gap is big, quiet parts can get lost. Loud parts may be too harsh on our ears.

But when there's too little contrast, the sound seems flat and dull.

Having the correct balance plays a big part in making songs exciting to listen to. A song with lots of ups and downs keeps us interested for longer periods than one that stays at the same volume.

This effect adds depth and emotion to the listening experience, taking it beyond simple sound reproduction into something more musical. 

The art of loudness and silence

Mastering the art of loudness and silence can profoundly affect your music's emotional depth. Dive into techniques for enhancing dynamics, discover how purposeful use of silence can amplify musical impact, and explore how to strike an effective balance between these elements.

Intrigued? Read on to elevate your understanding of this facet of music production!

Finding the right balance between loud and soft elements

Balancing loud and soft sounds in music is a true art. You give life to your music with this balance. Too much loudness can tire the ears. Same if your song has too little change between the quiet and loud parts.

The perfect blend makes your piece stand out. It keeps people hooked on every note, from start to finish! You measure these changes using decibels or dB for short. Most pop songs sit around 10 dB in dynamic range now.

But don't let that number limit you! Play around, test out different levels, and find what fits best with your style of music and the story you're sharing through sound. Most importantly, have fun doing it!

Techniques for emphasizing dynamics in music production

You can use many ways to boost dynamics in your music.

  1. Use audio compression: Audio engineers use this trick to control the dynamic range.
  2. Experiment with Yamaha AVENTAGE receivers: They make the dynamic range sound better.
  3. Avoid over-compression: Heavy compression makes a song too loud and hurts its dynamic range.
  4. Make use of Loudness Normalization: Streaming services use this method to keep loud and soft parts well-balanced.
  5. Aim for a wider dynamic range: The best pop music has a dynamic range of around 10 dB.

Another excellent way to enhance your music's dynamics is by using high-quality music production tools. SoundShockAudio offers a vast library of over 4,000 tools including VST Plugins, Sample Packs, DAW templates, MIDI files, Music Production Apps, Synth Presets, and Kontakt Instruments.

Creative use of silence to enhance musical impact

Silence in music can be a strong tool. It can make your song stand out. Too often, songs are loud from start to finish. This may cause listeners to lose interest quickly. Using silence smartly will keep them engaged and curious for more.

The pause could lead to a loud part, making the effect even stronger. Silence also brings out softer parts of your song, adding depth to it. But you need balance too! If used wrongly, silence might confuse your listeners or break the flow of your dynamic range in music.

Practice is key here! So next time you write a song, remember how important little breaks of quiet can be.

Conclusion

The art of loudness and silence shapes music. It makes a song exciting to listen to. It helps us enjoy the highs and lows in a tune. This is the magic of dynamic range in music.

Interested in elevating your music production skills? Don't miss our other blog post on effective tips for music production to further enhance your craft.

FAQs

1. What is the dynamic range in music?

Dynamic range in music is the difference between the softest and loudest sounds in a song.

2. Why is dynamic range important in music?

Dynamic range is important as it helps to create emotional impact and variety, making songs more engaging and interesting.

3. Can silence be part of the dynamic range?

Yes, silence can be a powerful part of the dynamic range, adding depth and drama to a piece of music.

4. How do musicians control dynamic range?

Musicians control dynamic range by altering how hard they play their instrument or adjusting volume levels during recording or mixing phases.

5. Does digital technology affect the dynamic range of music?

Yes, digital technology allows for precise control over the dynamic range but it can also lead to overly loud recordings if not used properly.

Yes, digital technology allows for precise control over the dynamic range but it can also lead to overly loud recordings if not used properly.

items.title: What is a MIDI keyboard and what is it used for?
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining factual accuracy.
items.excerptBefore: Not all MIDI controllers are keyboards. Learn what a MIDI keyboard controller is, and see our top picks for musicians in 2022.
items.excerptAfter: Explore the world of MIDI keyboards and controllers, their features, and top picks for musicians and live performances.
items.seoTitleBefore: What is a MIDI keyboard and what is it used for?
items.seoTitleAfter: What is a MIDI Keyboard and Its Uses
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Not all MIDI controllers are keyboards. Learn what a MIDI keyboard controller is, and see our top picks for musicians in 2022.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Learn about MIDI keyboards, their features, and discover our top picks for musicians and live performances.
items.beforeBody:
What is a MIDI keyboard, controller, and MIDI board?

It is a device that you can connect to a computer and all differ in size to transform computer-based plugins into an entirely hands-on playing experience. It provides real-time control and feedback of all virtual instruments.

Sturdy vs. light-weight

Some MIDI boards are made for portability, which is why they are extremely light and made out of plastic. But the downside is this can take away its sturdiness.

Features

MIDI keyboards come in various sizes, usually with either 25, 49, or 88 keys. Most models offer multiple key configurations, so you can pick the best one for your needs and portability requirements. For example, M-AUDIO’s popular model Keystation MK3 comes with 88 keys, or you can choose their 49-Key or their Mini 32 MK3.

Software

All MIDI boards are reliant on a computer and software in order to produce a sound. Most of the keyboards come with free software.

Editable functions

The number of variable control knobs depends on what you want to use it for. If you want to use it for performance, it's better to have more knobs directly accessible to you during the show. Look out for pitch and modulation wheels. Expression pedals and footswitch inputs on the back of the keyboard for even more control.

Nice to have features

Some keyboards have very sensitive keyboard aftertouch, which will allow you to express the tiniest details.

Here are our top picks for MIDI keyboards for live performances and musicians:

With these MIDI keyboards, users can play and create music with much freedom and ease.

1. Akai Professional Advance 49

The Akai Professional Advance 49 often gets compared to the cheaper version of the Komplete Kontrol MIDI board. You can use it as a stand-alone keyboard for a live gig using a MIDI out port and a power adapter.

Why we love it

  • USB Powered 
  • Pitch bend and modulation wheel, 8 velocity- and pressure-sensitive pads
  • Great browsing user experience

Cons

  • Slow on the software update
  • Few problems working with Logic Pro
Price: from US$260

2. Genki Wave MIDI ring

A one of its kind gestural MIDI ring controller. You can wear the Genki Wave on your finger and use natural gestures, moving your hand to control sound. This MIDI controller recognizes six gestures: Tilt, Pan, Roll, Vibrato, Tap, and Click.

Why we love it

  • Adds a humanistic touch to controlling and expressing yourself through your music
  • Creates a visual experience for audiences that adds a dramatic flair to performances
  • Feels natural and easy to integrate to music expression in real-time, with little obstruction (no more buttons and sliders)

Cons

  • There is a learning curve and users may find it more challenging at the beginning
  • Can be quite pricey for beginners
Price: US$249

3. Novation Launchkey 61 MK3 USB MIDI Keyboard

One of the most highly reviewed MIDI keyboards out there with a booming rating of 4.8 stars out of 5 on Amazon. They have options for 25 keys, 37 keys, 49 keys, and of course 61 keys. The best part about it is its Deep Ableton Live integration which allows you to easily access all the controls.

Why we love it

  • It has a Deep Ableton Live Integration - giving you immediate access to all the controls 
  • Fancy lights on pads
  • Works well even with Logic Pro x

Cons

    • Beginners might have a difficult time starting with it

Keys might feel cheap, as it's super light and shallow

Price: US$280

4. Native Instruments Komplete Kontrol A61

If you are already way deep in the Native Instrument ecosystem then this is for you. Native Instruments has several models for MIDI keyboards but if you are serious about music production then this is for you.

Why we love it

    • Sturdy build
    • USB-powered (fewer cables is always better)

Flawless DAW Integration Transport functions, knobs are automatically mapped and it just works.

Cons

  • Buttons may be clicky 
  • Reports of Logic Pro X incompatibility
Find Out More

5. M-AUDIO Keystation 61

One of the cheapest yet highly reviewed MIDI keyboard. It comes in 49 keys, 61, and 88 Keys. We love it for its simple layout - it removes all the pads and sliders which gives you that pro feeling. It has both a pitch and modulation slider.

Why we love it

  • Easy plug and play
  • Really cheap for what it can do
  • Very rugged and portable, won’t take up too much space

Cons

  • The keyboard may flex quite a bit as you play
Find Out More

6. Komplete Kontrol s61

Once you’ve experienced the Native Instrument environment we feel like there is no going back. Komplete Kontrol users usually do an upgrade with S61. It has pro-grade Fatar keybeds with aftertouch - 61 semi-weighted keys with pitch and mod wheels. With pre-mapped control of Komplete instruments and hundreds of Native Kontrol Standard (NKS) instruments.

Why we love it

  • Visual Feedback with two high-res colour screens
  • Integrates well with Maschine MK3 and Maschine software
  • Great keyboard

Cons

  • Key travel is long
Price: US$779

7. M-Audio Oxygen Pro

If you’re serious about music and want a keyboard that can do it all, the Oxygen Pro is for you. M-Audio’s highest-end offering comes with semi-weighted keys, meaning that it feels and plays like a real piano. Playing on the Oxygen Pro feels much more realistic and accurate compared to using a cheaper keyboard with hollow keys. 

You also get 16 customizable RGB-lit pads and 8 assignable knobs, along with 9 assignable faders and the usual pitch and modulation wheels. What’s even better is that the Oxygen Pro features Auto-Mapping, which means you can instantly map all the keyboard’s controls to your DAW without having to individually assign each function. 

The Oxygen Pro is available in a variety of key configurations, including the most popular 49 and 88-key versions.

Why we love it

  • Seamless DAW integration
  • Shortcut modes for easier music alternative
  • One of the cheapest option

Cons

Takes time to get used to

Shortcut modes for easier music alternative

Price: US$229

8. Arturia Keystep 37

Besides being a MIDI keyboard, Arturia’s Keystep 37 also functions as a sequencer, which means you can hook it up to your hardware synthesizers. This is great for musicians and producers who want to use more than just a keyboard to create their tracks.

Why we love it

  • Overall great sequencer
  • Perfect amount of keys

Cons

    • Might be too expensive
    Price: US$199

    Bonus MIDI controllers!

    1. Playtron

    The Playtron MIDI Controller offers a unique way to create your own custom MIDI controller. It has the ability to connect up to 16 objects with alligator clips. This allows you to use it with several mobile apps, online synthesizers, or your favourite DAW.

    From fruits to plants, and even toys, the possibilities are endless. Connect the clips from objects capable of conducting electricity to Playtron, and assign a different MIDI note (C1 to D#2 by default) to each object. Then, you can start playing and creating your own unique sounds!

    Why we love it

        • A unique way to make music and you could add a very interesting/interactive aspect to your performance
        • Very affordable price for the several versatile features
        • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances. 
        • Very portable (can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup)

    Cons

        • Not best for very precise control
    Price: US$95

    2. TouchMe

    TouchMe allows you to connect to objects in the same way as Playtron. However, this is a sensory device as it is set to react differently to the touch. Meaning, when the area of touch and intensity changes, the sound changes too, causing the pitch to go higher or lower!

    Why we love it

        • Overall great sequencer
        • Perfect amount of keys
        • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances. 
        • Explore the theremin-like sound generation potential of the TouchMe device to create dynamic and expressive music. 
        • Interact with other people in a musical creative environment.
        • Very portable (can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup)

    Cons

        • Not best for very precise control
    Price: US$99

What is a MIDI keyboard, controller, and MIDI board?

A MIDI keyboard is a device that connects to a computer, transforming computer-based plugins into a hands-on playing experience. It provides real-time control and feedback for all virtual instruments.

Sturdy vs. lightweight

Some MIDI boards are designed for portability, making them extremely lightweight and often made of plastic. However, this can compromise their sturdiness.

Features

MIDI keyboards come in various sizes, typically with 25, 49, or 88 keys. Most models offer multiple key configurations, allowing you to choose the best option for your needs and portability requirements. Software

All MIDI boards rely on a computer and software to produce sound. Most keyboards come with free software.

Editable functions

The number of variable control knobs varies based on your intended use. For performance, having more knobs readily accessible is beneficial. Look for pitch and modulation wheels, as well as expression pedals and footswitch inputs on the back of the keyboard for additional control.

Nice to have features

Some keyboards feature sensitive keyboard aftertouch, allowing for expressive playing and nuanced details.

Top picks for MIDI keyboards for live performances and musicians

These MIDI keyboards enable users to play and create music with freedom and ease.

1. Akai Professional Advance 49

The Akai Professional Advance 49 is often compared to the more affordable Komplete Kontrol MIDI board. It can be used as a standalone keyboard for live gigs via a MIDI out port and a power adapter.

This unique gestural MIDI ring controller can be worn on your finger, allowing you to control sound through natural gestures. The Genki Wave recognizes six gestures: Tilt, Pan, Roll, Vibrato, Tap, and Click.

  • Adds a humanistic touch to music control and expression
  • Creates a visual experience for audiences, enhancing performances
  • Integrates naturally into music expression in real-time with minimal obstruction

Cons

  • Can be pricey for newcomers

Why we love it

  • Deep Ableton Live integration for immediate control access
  • Illuminated pads
  • Compatible with Logic Pro X

Cons

  • Beginners may find it challenging to start
  • Keys may feel cheap due to their lightweight design

Price: US$280

4. Native Instruments Komplete Kontrol A61

If you are already invested in the Native Instruments ecosystem, this keyboard is ideal. It is designed for serious music production.

Why we love it

  • Sturdy build
  • USB powered for fewer cables
  • Flawless DAW integration with automatic mapping

Cons

  • Buttons may be clicky
  • Reports of incompatibility with Logic Pro X

Price: Find out more

5. M-AUDIO Keystation 61

This affordable and highly reviewed MIDI keyboard comes in 49, 61, and 88 key versions. It features a simple layout, removing pads and sliders for a professional feel, and includes pitch and modulation sliders.

Why we love it

  • Easy plug-and-play setup
  • Affordable for its capabilities
  • Rugged and portable design

Cons

  • The keyboard may flex during play

Price: Find out more

6. Komplete Kontrol S61

Once you experience the Native Instruments environment, upgrading to the S61 is common. It features pro-grade Fatar keybeds with aftertouch, 61 semi-weighted keys, and pre-mapped control for Komplete instruments.

Why we love it

  • Visual feedback with two high-resolution color screens
  • Integrates well with Maschine MK3 and Maschine software
  • Excellent keyboard quality

Cons

  • Long key travel

Price: US$779

7. M-Audio Oxygen Pro

If you’re serious about music and want a versatile keyboard, the Oxygen Pro is for you. It features semi-weighted keys for a realistic piano feel and includes 16 customizable RGB-lit pads, 8 assignable knobs, and 9 assignable faders.

The Oxygen Pro also features Auto-Mapping, allowing instant mapping of controls to your DAW.

Available in various key configurations, including popular 49 and 88-key versions.

Why we love it

  • Seamless DAW integration
  • Shortcut modes for easier music creation
  • Affordable for its features

Cons

It may take time to get used to.

Price: US$229

8. Arturia Keystep 37

Besides being a MIDI keyboard, Arturia’s Keystep 37 also functions as a sequencer, allowing you to connect it to hardware synthesizers. This is ideal for musicians and producers looking to expand their creative options.

Why we love it

  • Great sequencer capabilities
  • Perfect key count for versatility

Cons

  • May be considered expensive

Price: US$199

Bonus MIDI controllers

1. Playtron

The Playtron MIDI Controller allows you to create a custom MIDI controller by connecting up to 16 objects with alligator clips. This enables use with various mobile apps, online synthesizers, or your favorite DAW.

You can connect conductive objects like fruits, plants, or toys to Playtron, assigning different MIDI notes to each object. This allows for unique sound creation.

Why we love it

  • Offers a unique and interactive way to make music
  • Affordable for its versatile features
  • Can be combined with other MIDI controllers for complex arrangements
  • Portable with Bluetooth connectivity for wireless setups

Cons

  • Not ideal for precise control

Price: US$95

2. TouchMe

TouchMe connects to objects similarly to Playtron but is a sensory device that reacts to touch. Changes in touch area and intensity alter the sound, affecting pitch.

Why we love it

  • Great for exploring dynamic and expressive music
  • Encourages interaction in a musical creative environment
  • Portable with Bluetooth connectivity for wireless setups

Cons

  • Not ideal for precise control

Price: US$99

items.title: 10 piano accessories and tools every piano player needs to have
items.editorNotes: The article was rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original content and links.
items.excerptBefore: From cleaning and tuning tools to emergency supplies, discover all the essential piano accessories that every pianist needs to have,
items.excerptAfter: Discover the essential piano accessories and tools every pianist needs to enhance their playing experience. From metronomes to earplugs, this guide covers the top 10 must-haves for both beginners and seasoned players.
items.seoTitleBefore: 10 piano accessories and tools every piano player needs to have
items.seoTitleAfter: 10 Essential Piano Accessories Every Pianist Needs
items.seoDescriptionBefore: From cleaning and tuning tools to emergency supplies, discover all the essential piano accessories that every pianist needs to have,
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the top 10 must-have piano accessories and tools that every pianist should have. Enhance your practice and performances with these essential items.
items.beforeBody:
Whether you're a seasoned performer or a budding pianist and music enthusiast, having the right tools at your fingertips can make all the difference. It can take your piano playing to the next level. Imagine having a compact set of essential piano accessories in your gig bag, ready to support you in any situation.

That's why we've curated a list of the top 10 must-have piano accessories and tools that you can't miss.

1. Soundbrenner's wearable metronomes

Core 2 Pulse Core Steel

A compact metronome will help you stay on beat and maintain the rhythm, whether you're practicing or performing on a piano. Soundbrenner metronomes Core 2 and Pulse redefine musical practice. These innovative gadgets offer a vibrating metronome that you can feel rather than hear, ensuring your timing is always on point.

With a contact tuner for precise tuning and a dB meter to protect your hearing, these devices also double as smartwatches to keep you updated. Make your practice sessions more engaging and goal-oriented.

2. Tuning hammer

Buy now

When it comes to achieving the perfect pitch, a tuning hammer is your best friend. Keep it handy in your piano accessories kit to make on-the-spot adjustments before an important performance.

3. Minuendo earplugs

Buy now

Rehearsals and performances often take place in environments which might be very loud. Ear plugs can protect your hearing and help you concentrate on playing your piano, no matter the surroundings.

Minuendo lossless earplugs are essential for any musician, even more so for pianists. They are the world's first-ever variable passive hi-fi earplugs with stepless adjusters. You're able to effortlessly slide between a range of -7dB to -25 dB protection. These plugs safeguard your ears from sound levels up to 120 dB without ever needing a battery charge. Adjust your hearing protection on the go, all while experiencing no compromise on sound quality or comfort.

4. Cleaning supplies

Buy now

A clean piano is a happy piano! Tuck in a microfiber cloth and a small bottle of gentle piano cleaner in your piano accessories kit to ensure your instrument stays pristine and ready to impress.

5. Stomp Bluetooth foot pedal

Buy now

Introduce a touch of innovation to your piano practice and performances with Stomp, the #1 rated Bluetooth foot pedal designed to take your musical experience to new heights. This hands-free marvel allows you to control your favorite music apps with a simple tap of your foot. Built to last, its US-manufactured metal enclosure is durable enough to withstand any gigging environment.

Stomp features six silent, customizable buttons that provide tactile feedback, enabling you to swipe through chord charts or sheet music, and even start or stop your metronome seamlessly. Power will never be an issue with Stomp’s multiple power options; it runs for over 150 hours on a single 9V battery or can be powered via a 9VDC adapter.

Moreover, you can even charge your devices with its built-in USB port. With a 50-foot range and compatibility with any Bluetooth 4.0 enabled device, Stomp is an indispensable piano accessory that every pianist should consider.

6. Sheet music stand

Buy now

Bid farewell to the awkward balancing act of holding sheet music while playing. A collapsible sheet music stand is a game-changing piano accessory that ensures your focus remains solely on the keys of your piano.

7. Portable amp

Buy now

Not all digital pianos and keyboards have powerful built-in speakers (some don’t have any). A portable amp is a good choice for those who need a little extra volume to be heard over a band or other instruments. They’re also an excellent option for pianists who want to be able to practice or perform without being tethered to a plug.

8. Finger exercisers

Maintaining dexterity and strength is vital for pianists. Finger exercisers can be easily stashed in your gig bag, allowing you to warm up and keep those fingers nimble. You can also refer to our blog on effective finger stretches. Although it is written for guitarists, it is applicable for piano players as well.

Buy now

9. Foldable piano stool:

Buy now

A musician’s mind may compel him at any time and anywhere to set out his instrument and begin to play. In that case, having a stool in your piano accessories kit can be very helpful. You can just take it out of your gig bag, set up your piano, and begin playing anywhere you want to.

10. Piano light

Buy now

You've now unlocked the secrets to a well-prepared and stress-free collection of piano accessories. With these top 10 must-haves, you're equipped to face any challenge that comes your way during rehearsals, performances, or those unexpected jam sessions.

If you enjoyed this insightful article, you won't want to miss the wealth of knowledge and inspiration waiting for you in our other blog posts. Click here to check out our blog on top tips for learning the piano so you can master this wonderful instrument.

Whether you’re using an acoustic or a digital piano, the light is one of the most useful piano accessories you’ll find. Plus, you can use it for all those late-night sessions studying your fun, fun, fun music theory books!

If you enjoyed this article, check out our other blog posts for more insights. Click here for tips on learning the piano.

Whether you’re using an acoustic or a digital piano, a good light is one of the most useful accessories for late-night practice sessions.

Bonus MIDI controllers!

1. Playtron

The Playtron MIDI keyboards Controller offers a unique way to create your own custom MIDI controller. It has the ability to connect up to 16 objects with alligator clips. This allows you to use it with several mobile apps, online synthesizers, or your favorite DAW. 

From fruits to plants, and even toys, the possibilities are endless. Connect the clips from objects capable of conducting electricity to Playtron, and assign a different MIDI note (C1 to D#2 by default) to each object. Then, you can start playing and creating your own unique sounds!

Why we love it 

  • A unique way to make music, and you could add a very interesting/interactive aspect to your performance
  • Very affordable price for the several versatile features
  • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances.
  • Very portable (can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup)

Cons

  • Not best for very precise control
Price: US$95

2. TouchMe

TouchMe allows you to connect to objects in the same way as Playtron. However, this is a sensory device as it is set to react differently to the touch. Meaning, when the area of touch and intensity changes, the sound changes too, causing the pitch to go higher or lower! 

Why we love it

  • Overall great sequencer
  • Perfect amount of keys
  • Combine with other MIDI controllers or instruments to create complex arrangements and performances.
  • Explore the theremin-like sound generation potential of the TouchMe device to create dynamic and expressive music.
  • Interact with other people in a musical creative environment.
  • Very portable (can be connected to your computer, tablet, or smartphone via Bluetooth for a wireless setup)

Cons

  • Not best for very precise control
Price: US$99
items.afterBody:
What is a MIDI keyboard, controller, and MIDI board?

A MIDI keyboard, controller, or MIDI board is a device that connects to a computer, transforming computer-based plugins into a hands-on playing experience. It provides real-time control and feedback for all virtual instruments.

Sturdy vs. lightweight

Some MIDI boards are designed for portability, making them extremely light and often made of plastic. However, this can compromise their sturdiness.

Features

MIDI keyboards come in various sizes, typically with 25, 49, or 88 keys. Most models offer multiple key configurations, allowing you to choose the best one for your needs and portability requirements. For instance, M-AUDIO’s popular Keystation MK3 model is available with 88 keys, as well as 49-key and Mini 32 MK3 options.

Software

All MIDI boards rely on a computer and software to produce sound. Most keyboards come with free software.

Editable functions

The number of variable control knobs varies based on your intended use. For live performance, having more accessible knobs is beneficial. Look for pitch and modulation wheels, as well as expression pedals and footswitch inputs on the back of the keyboard for enhanced control.

Nice-to-have features

Some keyboards feature sensitive keyboard aftertouch, allowing for nuanced expression.

Here are our top picks for MIDI keyboards for live performances and musicians:

These MIDI keyboards enable users to play and create music with freedom and ease.

1. Akai Professional Advance 49

The Akai Professional Advance 49 is often compared to the more affordable Komplete Kontrol MIDI board. It can be used as a standalone keyboard for live gigs, thanks to its MIDI out port and power adapter.

Why we love it

  • USB powered
  • Pitch bend and modulation wheel, 8 velocity- and pressure-sensitive pads
  • Great browsing user experience

Cons

  • Slow software updates
  • Some issues with Logic Pro compatibility
Price: from US$260

2. Genki Wave MIDI ring

This unique gestural MIDI ring controller can be worn on your finger, allowing you to use natural gestures to control sound. The Genki Wave recognizes six gestures: Tilt, Pan, Roll, Vibrato, Tap, and Click.

Why we love it

  • Adds a humanistic touch to music control and expression
  • Creates a visual experience for audiences, enhancing performances
  • Feels natural and integrates seamlessly into real-time music expression

Cons

  • Learning curve may be challenging for new users
  • Can be pricey for beginners
Price: US$249

3. Novation Launchkey 61 MK3 USB MIDI Keyboard

Highly rated with 4.8 stars out of 5 on Amazon, the Novation Launchkey is available in 25, 37, 49, and 61 key options. Its deep integration with Ableton Live allows easy access to all controls.

Why we love it

  • Deep Ableton Live integration for immediate control access
  • Illuminated pads
  • Compatible with Logic Pro X

Cons

  • Beginners may find it difficult to start
  • Keys may feel cheap due to lightweight design
Price: US$280

4. Native Instruments Komplete Kontrol A61

If you are deeply integrated into the Native Instruments ecosystem, this keyboard is ideal. Native Instruments offers several MIDI keyboard models, but the A61 is perfect for serious music production.

Why we love it

  • Sturdy build
  • USB powered for fewer cables
  • Excellent DAW integration with automatic mapping

Cons

  • Buttons may be clicky
  • Reports of incompatibility with Logic Pro X
Find Out More

5. M-AUDIO Keystation 61

This affordable yet highly reviewed MIDI keyboard is available in 49, 61, and 88 key versions. Its simple layout, devoid of pads and sliders, provides a professional feel, complete with pitch and modulation sliders.

Why we love it

  • Easy plug-and-play functionality
  • Affordable for its capabilities
  • Rugged and portable design

Cons

  • The keyboard may flex while playing
Find Out More

6. Komplete Kontrol S61

Once you experience the Native Instrument environment, upgrading to the S61 is common. It features pro-grade Fatar key beds with aftertouch, 61 semi-weighted keys, pitch, and modulation wheels, along with pre-mapped controls for Komplete instruments.

Why we love it

  • Visual feedback with two high-resolution color screens
  • Integrates well with Maschine MK3 and Maschine software
  • Excellent keyboard quality

Cons

  • Long key travel
Price: US$779

7. M-Audio Oxygen Pro

If you’re serious about music and want a versatile keyboard, the Oxygen Pro is for you. M-Audio’s top-tier offering features semi-weighted keys, providing a realistic piano feel. It includes 16 customizable RGB-lit pads, 8 assignable knobs, 9 assignable faders, and pitch and modulation wheels. The Auto-Mapping feature allows instant mapping of controls to your DAW.

The Oxygen Pro is available in various key configurations, including popular 49 and 88-key versions.

Why we love it

  • Seamless DAW integration
  • Shortcut modes for easier music creation
  • One of the most affordable options

Cons

  • May take time to adjust to
  • Shortcut modes can be confusing
Price: US$229

8. Arturia Keystep 37

In addition to being a MIDI keyboard, Arturia’s Keystep 37 also functions as a sequencer, allowing you to connect it to hardware synthesizers. This is ideal for musicians and producers who want to create tracks beyond just using a keyboard.

Why we love it

  • Excellent sequencer capabilities
  • Ideal number of keys for versatility

Cons

  • May be considered expensive
Price: US$199

Bonus MIDI controllers!

1. Playtron

The Playtron MIDI Controller offers a unique way to create custom MIDI controllers. It can connect up to 16 objects using alligator clips, allowing you to use it with various mobile apps, online synthesizers, or your favorite DAW.

From fruits to plants and even toys, the possibilities are endless. Connect clips from conductive objects to Playtron and assign different MIDI notes (C1 to D#2 by default) to each object. Then, you can start creating unique sounds!

Why we love it

  • A unique approach to music creation, adding an interactive aspect to performances
  • Affordable for its versatile features
  • Can be combined with other MIDI controllers or instruments for complex arrangements
  • Highly portable, connects via Bluetooth for a wireless setup

Cons

  • Not ideal for precise control
Price: US$95

2. TouchMe

TouchMe connects to objects similarly to Playtron but is a sensory device that reacts differently to touch. Changes in touch area and intensity alter the sound, affecting pitch in real-time.

Why we love it

  • Great for creating dynamic and expressive music
  • Encourages interaction in a musical creative environment
  • Portable, connects via Bluetooth for a wireless setup

Cons

  • Not ideal for precise control
Price: US$99
